Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A basic cyber attack orchestrated by 'Operation Honeybee' targeting water and wastewater systems involves phishing emails to gain initial access, followed by executing destructive actions such as deploying ransomware, culminating in data manipulation to disrupt operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send phishing emails to employees, attempting to trick them into clicking on malicious links or opening infected attachments.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, the attackers deliver malware which upon execution begins to destroy files on the compromised systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 153; Name: Input Data Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers manipulate system processes to alter critical data, causing operational disruption without requiring deep technical expertise.

Scenario.summary: Operation Honeybee employs basic phishing techniques to infiltrate water and wastewater systems, leading to data destruction and manipulation, thereby disrupting services. This attack leverages the human element as the weakest link, exploiting minimal technical skill.
